name: Martini
tagline: The film set for AI videos — direct entire productions from your agent.
description: Martini is the command surface for agentic filmmaking: one MCP connection that puts an entire film set — the latest video models, infinite production canvases, consistent characters, and a real timeline — under your agent's direction. It is an open platform, agent-friendly by design: the MCP server is a first-class product surface rather than an integration afterthought, so any MCP-capable agent (Claude, Cursor, or one you built) gets the same full control of the set that the UI offers. The model roster stays current (Veo 3.1, Sora 2 Pro, Kling 3.0 Pro and O3 Pro, Hailuo 02, Seedance 1.5, Flux 2 Max, GPT Image 2, CassetteAI sound effects, and more), and the server resolves natural-language model requests to the right engine. Sixty-plus tools span the pipeline: storyboard nodes and batch shot generation with cost approval, reusable subjects with costumes for character and prop consistency across shots, prompt variables for shared style control, frame extraction for shot chaining, asset upload and download, multi-track timeline editing with ripple and overwrite semantics, and real-time collaborative canvases your whole team sees live. Over 200 professional films have been made on Martini — aired as TV commercials, shown as artwork, and gone viral.

auth.key_url: https://www.martini.film — sign in with your Martini account

notes: Remote MCP server maintained first-party by Martini (YC-backed), which treats agents as first-class users: humans and agents share the same canvases in real time, so an agent can rough in a board overnight and a director can polish it in the morning. The breadth is the point — where most listings here cover one model or one pipeline stage, Martini's single connection covers develop-to-cut: agents plan boards, generate across the full model roster, keep characters consistent with subjects and costumes, and assemble timelines without leaving the server. Generation tools require explicit user approval of model, prompt, and estimated cost before spending credits — a deliberate guardrail for agent-driven production. Connect instructions: martini.film/docs/mcp.
